---
title: "Interactive Career Roadmap: Jonathan's Path to Oncolytic Virus R&D Leadership"
jupyter: python3
---

# 🚀 Overview & Quick Reference

## PhD Prerequisite Courses & Provider

Classes required for PhD programs, focusing on core chemistry courses: 

- [ ] Organic Chemistry I & II (Retake I)
- [ ] Biochemistry

**Top Choice Provider: UC San Diego Extension**
- **Courses**: Organic Chemistry I & II, Biochemistry
- **Cost**: \$845 per course (4.5 units each)
- **Format**: Asynchronous, regionally accredited, 10-12 weeks/course.
- **Website**: [extendedstudies.ucsd.edu](https://extendedstudies.ucsd.edu)

## Continued Education Subjects

Subjects that are helpful for graduate studies and beyond, focusing on more specialized areas: 

- [ ] **Programming/Scripting**: Python, R, SQL, Bash, Git, Rust (if time permits)
- [ ] **Advanced Biology**: Molecular Virology, Cancer Biology, Tumor Immunology, Genomics, Physiology/Pharmacology/Histopathology
- [ ] **Mathematics**: Biostatistics, Linear Algebra, Calculus, Algebra, Trigonometry
- [ ] **Scientific Communication**: Grant/Proposal Writing, Literature Publication, Peer Review, 
- [ ] **Underrated Subjects**: Ethics & Psychology

**Read scientific literature, lots of it**. It's how scientists communicate their findings to each other. Master literature review.  

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Target PhD Programs

- **MD Anderson GSBS - Viroimmunotherapy**: [gsbs.uth.edu](https://gsbs.uth.edu/) (Best match)
- **Mayo Clinic - Virology & Gene Therapy**: [mayo.edu](https://mayo.edu/mayo-clinic-graduate-school) (Mainly wet lab)
- **City of Hope - Translational Medicine**: [cityofhope.org](https://cityofhope.org/education) (Great TM focus)
- **University of Pittsburgh - Cancer Biology**: [pitt.edu](https://gradbiomed.pitt.edu) (Strong cancer research)
- **UPenn BGS - Molecular Virology**: [med.upenn.edu](https://www.med.upenn.edu/bgs/) (Most prestige)
- **Others**: Utah/Huntsman, Stanford, Duke, MSK.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Milestone Timeline (2025-2040)

| Age | Year | Key Milestones |
| ------------------ | ------------------ | ----------------------------------- |
| 26 | Apr 2026 | Graduate BYU, Start industry lab job |
| 27-28 | 2026-2027 | Complete Ochem + Biochemistry |
| 29-30 | 2028-2029 | MS Translational Bioinformatics (Utah) while working |
| 30 | Dec 2029 | Graduate with MS, ~4 years lab experience |
| 30-31 | 2029-2030 | PhD application cycle |
| 31 | Aug 2030 | Begin PhD in Oncolytic Virotherapy |
| 35-37 | 2035-2036 | Complete PhD, enter industry as Scientist |
| 40+ | 2040+ | Senior Scientist / Director + Adjunct Professor |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Financial Strategy Summary

| Category | Investment | Timeline |
| ------------------ | ------------------ | ------------------ |
| **Chemistry Prerequisites** | $1,690 - $2,535 | 2026-2027 |
| **Subject Specializations**| $200 - $300 | 2026-2027 |
| **MS Bioinformatics (Utah)**| $30,000 - $35,000 | 2028-2029 |
| **Total Training Cost** | **~$32,000 - $38,000** | **Spread over 4 years** |

- **Income (2026-2030)**: $55,000-70,000/year industry lab salary.
- **Savings Goals**: Save $30k+ during Phase 1 for MS tuition; save for PhD relocation during Phase 2.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 🧘 Strategic Guidelines

- **Sustainability > Speed**: This is a 10+ year journey. A 3.7 GPA is better than burnout.
- **Employer Benefits**: Take courses **after starting full-time work** (May 2026+) to leverage potential tuition assistance.
- **Networking**: Email admissions at target programs early to confirm prerequisite requirements and build rapport.
- **Boundaries**: Limit grad applications to 2-3 hours/day max to prevent "app fatigue."
- **Lab Culture**: Prioritize lab culture and PI mentorship over institutional prestige when selecting a PhD lab.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 📚 Detailed Career Phases

**Interactive Tool for Planning & Tracking Career Milestones (2025-2040)**
Use the `<details>` blocks below for deep-dive strategy, then use the Python tool at the bottom for dynamic planning.

<details>
<summary><b>PHASE 0 - Finishing Strong at BYU (Nov 2025 - Apr 2026)</b></summary>

### Academic Priorities
- **Target GPA**: Push for 3.4+ cumulative GPA (currently 3.36).
- **Key Courses** (Winter 2026): 
  - **Virology** (MMBIO 465)
  - **Bioinformatics** (BIO 165)
  - **Bacterial Pathogenesis & Physiology** (MMBIO 364 + 461)
- **Undergraduate Research**: Continue MMBIO 494R and CHEM 497R. Aim for 2 years total diverse lab experience.
- **Skill Goal**: Gain hands-on **qPCR** & **Flow Cytometry** proficiency (high value for cancer research).

</details>

<details>
<summary><b>PHASE 1 - Industry Lab Work + Prerequisites (Apr 2026 - Dec 2027)</b></summary>

**Timeline**: 1.5-2 years | **Goal**: Build professional lab expertise + fix chemistry grades.

### Employment: Research Associate / Lab Technician
- **Target Employers**: ARUP, Myriad Genetics, Recursion, Huntsman Cancer Institute, or U of U Core Facilities.
- **Salary Range**: $50,000-70,000.
- **Why Industry?**: Predictable 9-5 schedule allows dedicated time for UCSD coursework and self-study.

### Academic Prerequisites (UCSD Extension)
- **Priority 1**: Retake **Organic Chemistry 1** (Summer 2026). Replace 'D' grade with 'B+' or higher.
- **Priority 2**: Complete **Biochemistry** (Fall 2026).
- **Priority 3**: **Organic Chemistry 2** (Spring 2027 - if required by target programs).

### Self-Study & Foundation
- **Coursera**: Python for Genomic Data Science, R Programming, Cancer Biology Specialization (Johns Hopkins).
- **Math**: Statistics review (StatQuest), Linear Algebra (3Blue1Brown).
- **Literature**: Weekly reading of *Nature Immunology* and *Nature Cancer*. 
- **Textbooks**: See booklist in [[Jonathan_Learning_Map]].

</details>

<details>
<summary><b>PHASE 2 - MS Translational Bioinformatics + Industry (Jan 2028 - Dec 2029)</b></summary>

**Timeline**: 2 years | **Goal**: MS degree + ~4 years cumulative lab experience.

### MS at University of Utah (Translational Bioinformatics Track)
- **Format**: Fully online/part-time for professionals.
- **Focus**: Python/R, statistical modeling, machine learning, data visualization, precision medicine.
- **Capstone Strategy**: 
  - Partner with UofU/Huntsman researchers. Aim for a translational focus.
  - Focus: "ML Prediction of Oncolytic Virus Response" or "Computational Analysis of Tumor-Virus Interactions."
  - Utilize TCGA, GEO, and cBioPortal datasets. Goal: Publish in a peer-reviewed journal.

### Industry Growth
- Continue full-time lab work to reach ~4 years of professional experience by PhD application time.
- Network with Utah/Huntsman faculty to build an "inside track" for PhD admissions.

</details>

<details>
<summary><b>PHASE 3 - PhD Applications (June 2029 - April 2030)</b></summary>

**Timeline**: 1 year cycle | **Goal**: Secure admission to a top oncolytic virotherapy lab.

- **Target List**: MD Anderson GSBS, Mayo Clinic, Utah/Huntsman, City of Hope, UPenn, University of Pittsburgh.
- **Application Profile**:
  - BS Microbiology + MS Translational Bioinformatics (Target GPA 3.7+).
  - 4 years professional lab experience.
  - Dual-threat expertise: Wet lab (Flow Cytometry/Cell Culture) + Dry lab (Python/R/ML).
- **Letters**: Utah MS advisor (computational), BYU research mentor, and Industry supervisor.

</details>

<details>
<summary><b>PHASE 4 - PhD Training (2030 - 2035/36)</b></summary>

**Timeline**: 5-6 years | **Goal**: Become a global expert in viral-tumor interactions.

- **Year 1**: Rotations in viral engineering, cancer immunology, and computational biology.
- **Year 2**: Select thesis lab (oncolytic focus), pass oral quals, publish first co-authored paper.
- **Years 3-5**: Execute dissertation research, apply for **NIH F31** predoctoral fellowship, present at AACR/SITC.
- **Graduation**: Target 3-5 first-author publications.

</details>

<details>
<summary><b>PHASE 5 - Industry Career & Leadership (2035/36+)</b></summary>

**Timeline**: Long-term | **Goal**: Lead R&D programs + Adjunct Teaching.

- **Years 1-3 (Scientist I/II)**: Bridge discovery to clinical trials.
- **Years 4-8 (Senior Scientist)**: Manage cross-functional teams.
- **Years 9+ (Director/Principal Scientist)**: Strategic pipeline leadership.
- **Adjunct Teaching**: Teach Virology or Cancer Biology at a local university (1-2 courses/year).

</details>

<details>
<summary><b>Contingency Plans & Advantages</b></summary>

- **Competitive Edge**: Most PhDs lack computational sophistication; most data scientists lack wet lab proficiency. You have both.
- **Maturity**: Entering a PhD at 31 with 4 years of industry experience provides superior project management and stability.
- **Pivot Plan**: If PhD admissions are delayed, the MS in Bioinformatics provides immediate eligibility for high-paying Computational Biology roles.

</details>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 💻 Code Cells

```{python}
# Import required libraries
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.express as px
from datetime import datetime
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta
import ipywidgets as widgets
from IPython.display import display, Markdown

print("✅ Libraries imported successfully!")
```

## 📊 Part 1: Timeline Configuration

```{python}
# ========== ADJUSTABLE PARAMETERS ==========
BIRTH_YEAR = 1999
GRADUATION_DATE = datetime(2026, 4, 1)

# Durations
PHASE1_DURATION_MONTHS = 21 
MS_DURATION_YEARS = 2  
PHD_DURATION_YEARS = 5.5  
SKIP_POSTDOC = True
POSTDOC_DURATION_YEARS = 2

# Finances
LAB_STARTING_SALARY = 55000 
LAB_ANNUAL_RAISE_PCT = 5.0
MS_ANNUAL_TUITION = 15000
PHD_STIPEND = 42000
SCIENTIST_STARTING_SALARY = 125000

current_age = datetime.now().year - BIRTH_YEAR
graduation_age = GRADUATION_DATE.year - BIRTH_YEAR

print(f"📋 Configuration loaded! Current age: {current_age}")
```

## 🗓️ Part 2: Calculate Timeline Milestones

```{python}
def calculate_age(date, birth_year=BIRTH_YEAR):
    return date.year - birth_year

def calculate_timeline():
    milestones = {}
    milestones['graduation'] = GRADUATION_DATE
    milestones['graduation_age'] = calculate_age(GRADUATION_DATE)
    
    phase1_start = GRADUATION_DATE
    phase1_end = phase1_start + relativedelta(months=PHASE1_DURATION_MONTHS)
    milestones['phase1_start'] = phase1_start
    milestones['phase1_end'] = phase1_end
    
    ms_start = phase1_end + relativedelta(months=1)
    ms_end = ms_start + relativedelta(months=int(MS_DURATION_YEARS * 12))
    milestones['ms_start'] = ms_start
    milestones['ms_end'] = ms_end
    milestones['ms_end_age'] = calculate_age(ms_end)
    
    lab_years = (ms_end - phase1_start).days / 365.25
    milestones['total_lab_years'] = round(lab_years, 1)
    
    phd_apply_start = ms_end - relativedelta(months=6)
    phd_start = ms_end + relativedelta(months=8)
    phd_end = phd_start + relativedelta(months=int(PHD_DURATION_YEARS * 12))
    milestones['phd_apply_start'] = phd_apply_start
    milestones['phd_start'] = phd_start
    milestones['phd_end'] = phd_end
    milestones['phd_start_age'] = calculate_age(phd_start)
    milestones['phd_end_age'] = calculate_age(phd_end)
    
    if SKIP_POSTDOC:
        career_start = phd_end + relativedelta(months=2)
        milestones['postdoc'] = False
    else:
        postdoc_start = phd_end + relativedelta(months=2)
        postdoc_end = postdoc_start + relativedelta(months=int(POSTDOC_DURATION_YEARS * 12))
        career_start = postdoc_end + relativedelta(months=2)
        milestones['postdoc'] = True
        milestones['postdoc_start'] = postdoc_start
        milestones['postdoc_end'] = postdoc_end

    milestones['industry_start'] = career_start
    milestones['industry_start_age'] = calculate_age(career_start)
    
    milestones['senior_scientist'] = career_start + relativedelta(years=5)
    milestones['senior_scientist_age'] = calculate_age(milestones['senior_scientist'])
    milestones['principal_scientist'] = career_start + relativedelta(years=10)
    milestones['principal_scientist_age'] = calculate_age(milestones['principal_scientist'])
    
    return milestones

timeline = calculate_timeline()
print(f"✅ MS completion: {timeline['ms_end'].strftime('%B %Y')} (Age {timeline['ms_end_age']})")
print(f"✅ PhD completion: {timeline['phd_end'].strftime('%B %Y')} (Age {timeline['phd_end_age']})")
```

## 📈 Part 3: Interactive Timeline Visualization

```{python}
def create_gantt_chart(timeline):
    phases = [
        dict(Task="Phase 0: Finishing BYU", Start=datetime.now(), Finish=timeline['graduation'], Resource="Education"),
        dict(Task="Phase 1: Lab Work + Prereqs", Start=timeline['phase1_start'], Finish=timeline['phase1_end'], Resource="Work"),
        dict(Task="Phase 2: MS Bioinformatics", Start=timeline['ms_start'], Finish=timeline['ms_end'], Resource="Work + MS"),
        dict(Task="Phase 3: PhD Virotherapy", Start=timeline['phd_start'], Finish=timeline['phd_end'], Resource="Education"),
        dict(Task="Phase 4: Industry Scientist", Start=timeline['industry_start'], Finish=timeline['senior_scientist'], Resource="Career"),
        dict(Task="Senior Scientist Level", Start=timeline['senior_scientist'], Finish=timeline['principal_scientist'], Resource="Career")
    ]
    
    df = pd.DataFrame(phases)
    fig = px.timeline(df, x_start="Start", x_end="Finish", y="Task", color="Resource", title="Career Roadmap Timeline (2025-2045)")
    fig.update_yaxes(autorange="reversed")
    fig.update_layout(height=500)
    return fig

create_gantt_chart(timeline).show()
```

## 💰 Part 4: Financial Projections

```{python}
def calculate_finances(timeline):
    financial_data = []
    current_salary = LAB_STARTING_SALARY
    
    # Phase 1 & 2
    for year in range(timeline['phase1_start'].year, timeline['phd_start'].year):
        in_ms = timeline['ms_start'].year <= year < timeline['ms_end'].year
        tuition = MS_ANNUAL_TUITION if in_ms else 0
        financial_data.append({'Year': year, 'Age': year - BIRTH_YEAR, 'Income': current_salary, 'Tuition': tuition, 'Net': current_salary - tuition})
        current_salary *= (1 + LAB_ANNUAL_RAISE_PCT / 100)
    
    # Phase 3
    for year in range(timeline['phd_start'].year, timeline['phd_end'].year + 1):
        financial_data.append({'Year': year, 'Age': year - BIRTH_YEAR, 'Income': PHD_STIPEND, 'Tuition': 0, 'Net': PHD_STIPEND})
        
    df_finance = pd.DataFrame(financial_data)
    df_finance['Cumulative_Net'] = df_finance['Net'].cumsum()
    return df_finance

df_finances = calculate_finances(timeline)
display(df_finances.head(10))
```

## 🎮 Part 5: Interactive Timeline Adjuster

```{python}
# Create interactive widgets
style = {'description_width': '200px'}

phase1_slider = widgets.IntSlider(value=PHASE1_DURATION_MONTHS, min=12, max=36, step=3, description='Phase 1 Duration (mo):', style=style)
ms_slider = widgets.FloatSlider(value=MS_DURATION_YEARS, min=2.0, max=5.0, step=0.5, description='MS Duration (yr):', style=style)
phd_slider = widgets.FloatSlider(value=PHD_DURATION_YEARS, min=4.5, max=7.0, step=0.5, description='PhD Duration (yr):', style=style)

output = widgets.Output()

def update_timeline(change):
    global PHASE1_DURATION_MONTHS, MS_DURATION_YEARS, PHD_DURATION_YEARS
    PHASE1_DURATION_MONTHS = phase1_slider.value
    MS_DURATION_YEARS = ms_slider.value
    PHD_DURATION_YEARS = phd_slider.value
    
    new_timeline = calculate_timeline()
    with output:
        output.clear_output(wait=True)
        print(f"🔄 MS Completion: {new_timeline['ms_end'].strftime('%B %Y')} (Age {new_timeline['ms_end_age']})")
        print(f"🔄 PhD Completion: {new_timeline['phd_end'].strftime('%B %Y')} (Age {new_timeline['phd_end_age']})")
        print(f"🔄 Total Lab Exp: {new_timeline['total_lab_years']} years")

for w in [phase1_slider, ms_slider, phd_slider]:
    w.observe(update_timeline, 'value')

display(widgets.VBox([widgets.HTML("<h3>🎯 Adjust Timeline</h3>"), phase1_slider, ms_slider, phd_slider, output]))
update_timeline(None)
```

## 📋 Part 6: Milestone Checklist

```{python}
milestones_checklist = {
    "Phase 0: BYU": ["3.4+ GPA", "Virology (MMBIO 465)", "Bioinformatics (BIO 165)", "Lab Research"],
    "Phase 1: Lab + Prereqs": ["Secure Job", "Ochem 1 Retake", "Biochemistry", "Coursera Foundations"],
    "Phase 2: MS + Work": ["Utah MS Apply", "3.7+ GPA", "Capstone (Huntsman Partner)", "4y Lab Exp"],
    "Phase 3: PhD Apps": ["Research Labs", "Personal Statement", "Letters of Rec", "Accept Offer"]
}

checklist_widgets = []
for phase, items in milestones_checklist.items():
    checklist_widgets.append(widgets.HTML(f"<h4>{phase}</h4>"))
    for item in items:
        checklist_widgets.append(widgets.Checkbox(description=item, layout=widgets.Layout(width='100%')))

display(widgets.VBox(checklist_widgets))
```

## 📥 Part 7: Export Summary

```{python}
export_button = widgets.Button(description='📥 Export Timeline', button_style='success')
export_output = widgets.Output()

def on_export_click(b):
    with export_output:
        t = calculate_timeline()
        summary = f"Career Roadmap Summary\nMS Completion: {t['ms_end'].strftime('%B %Y')}\nPhD Start: {t['phd_start'].strftime('%B %Y')}\nTotal Lab Exp: {t['total_lab_years']} years"
        with open('roadmap_export.txt', 'w') as f:
            f.write(summary)
        print("✅ Exported to roadmap_export.txt")

export_button.on_click(on_export_click)
display(export_button, export_output)
```

---
*Last updated: March 6, 2026*
